songs.title: One Water, Two Souls
songs.hook: The most ordinary thing on Earth has been living a double life.

paper.title: Evidence for the generic existence of two local structures in liquid water (Li & Zeng, 2026, Nature Physics)

songs.story:
You have poured it, sipped it, and swum in it your whole life — and it turns out water was never just one thing. Physicists suspected for fifty years that liquid water hides two distinct molecular personalities: one crowd packed in tight (high-density water), and one holding itself open like a country road (low-density water), endlessly switching back and forth.
The proof finally came from an unexpected witness. Researchers handed an AI millions of unlabeled snapshots of water molecules — no hints, no answer sheet — and it found the two hidden neighborhoods on its own.
That quiet double life explains one of nature's best tricks: why water gets denser only down to 4 °C and then pushes back, which is why ice floats, lakes freeze from the top down, and life under the surface survives winter. One water, two souls.
